input.background: The Scandinavian simvastatin survival study (4S) was a randomized clinical trial designed to evaluate the effects of the cholesterol-lowering agent simvastatin in patients with coronary heart disease. Over 5.4 years of follow-up, the treatment group consisting of 2221 individuals experienced 111 fatal heart attacks. The placebo group of 2223 individuals experienced 189 such events.
input.question: In relative terms, how much did simvastatin lower heart attack mortality?

output.method: 分类或发生概率影响因素解读
output.answer: The easiest way to address this question is to first calculate the relative risk: $\widehat{R R}=\frac{0.049977}{0.085020}=0.59$. The relative reduction in risk $=1-0.59=0.41$, or $41 \%$.

treatment_group_size.description: The number of individuals in the treatment group who received simvastatin.

placebo_group_size.description: The number of individuals in the placebo group who did not receive simvastatin.

fatal_heart_attacks_treatment.description: The number of fatal heart attacks observed in the treatment group.

fatal_heart_attacks_placebo.description: The number of fatal heart attacks observed in the placebo group.

simvastatin_effect.description: The effect of simvastatin on heart attack mortality, to be calculated as the relative reduction in fatal heart attacks.
